can someone eplain to me how AfterShock works?
I mean, Corpo's Pilot says, Aftershock:gain str when discarded (or w/e it was exactly)
so if I discard the card I get that effect?
also, her Lightning has that aftershock effect as well that the card goes to the top of my deck, how does that work?
Correct, if the card is discarded, then the aftershock effect gets triggered.
For Lightning, that just means that if it gets discarded, then the next card you draw will be Lightning.
I have Corposant Hameln and horla now yay
This means you can use a discard effect to trigger its Prepare and reduce its Arithmetica cost, then use a draw effect to draw it again.
You can see this when you use her Exalt while you have Lightning in hand. You'll get back her lightning but with -1 cost
https://streamable.com/afizcq Both Lightning and No Trespassing has Prepare. When I use Aurita's discard card, you can see both cards getting reduced cost before they get discarded.
so that's not the work of retain?!
Retain only allows you to keep your cards in hand when you end your turn.
I see, and does ending the turn with the card in hand count as Discard?
When you end your turn, you enter discard phase. This means you discard all cards in hand except for cards with Retain.
You can see it in this video. When I end my turn, Tulu's Aftershock effect on his Strike triggers, causing tentacles to attack
But it's hard to notice since tentacles attack at end of turn anyways 🤷‍♂️
err. If they have retain, they don't get discarded, so in that case it doesn't count as discard.
